124	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
125	# Text utilities
126	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
127	
128	_WIKI_LINK_RE = re.compile(r'\[([^\]]+)\]\(wiki:[^)]+\)')
129	_CITE_REF_RE = re.compile(r'\(wiki:[^)]+\)')
130	_ANGLE_QUOTE_RE = re.compile(r'[«»]')
131	
132	
133	def _clean(text: str) -> str:
134	    """
135	    Strip wiki markup from snippet/paragraph text and return lowercased plain text.
136	    Removes: «»  [[Link|text]] style refs  (wiki:...) citation anchors
137	    """
138	    text = _WIKI_LINK_RE.sub(r'\1', text)
139	    text = _CITE_REF_RE.sub('', text)
140	    text = _ANGLE_QUOTE_RE.sub('', text)
141	    return text.lower()
142	
143	
144	def _t(type_str: str) -> str:
145	    """Return top-level type prefix, e.g. 'character' from 'character.human'."""
146	    return type_str.split(".")[0] if type_str else ""
147	
148	
149	def _source_name_parts(source_slug: str) -> list:
150	    """
151	    Return lowercased name fragments from a slug for snippet presence checks.
152	    E.g. 'podrick-payne' → ['podrick', 'payne']
153	         'amaury-peake' → ['amaury', 'peake']
154	    Filters out very short tokens (len < 4) to avoid false matches.
155	    """
156	    return [p for p in source_slug.split("-") if len(p) >= 4]
157	
158	
159	def _source_in_snippet(source_slug: str, snippet_clean: str) -> bool:
160	    """
161	    Return True if the source entity's FIRST NAME (first slug token with len>=4)
162	    appears in the cleaned snippet.
163	
164	    We intentionally use the FIRST name part only (not last/family name) because
165	    family names (e.g. 'peake') appear in the snippet for OTHER members of the
166	    same family, causing false positives. First names are generally unique enough
167	    within a paragraph window to serve as reliable presence indicators.
168	
169	    If the first significant token is absent but the whole slug is very short,
170	    fall back to any-match.
171	    """
172	    parts = _source_name_parts(source_slug)
173	    if not parts:
174	        return True  # slug too short — be permissive
175	    # Use only the FIRST significant name part as the discriminating token
176	    # (first name of the person), not the family name
177	    first_name = parts[0]
178	    return first_name in snippet_clean
179	
180	
181	def _split_snippet_at_target(raw_snippet: str) -> tuple:
182	    """
183	    Split the raw snippet into (before_target, target_text, after_target)
184	    using the «...» anchor markers. Returns cleaned (lowercased, markup-stripped)
185	    versions of each part.
186	
187	    If no «» markers found, returns (full_clean, '', '').
188	    """
189	    if "«" not in raw_snippet:
190	        return (_clean(raw_snippet), "", "")
191	
192	    # Find the first «...» span
193	    start = raw_snippet.find("«")
194	    end = raw_snippet.find("»", start)
195	    if end == -1:
196	        return (_clean(raw_snippet), "", "")
197	
198	    before = raw_snippet[:start]
199	    target = raw_snippet[start+1:end]
200	    after = raw_snippet[end+1:]
201	    return (_clean(before), _clean(target), _clean(after))
202	
203	
204	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
205	# Main classifier dispatch
206	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
207	
208	def classify_candidate(row: dict) -> dict:
209	    """
210	    Apply deterministic classification rules to one candidate row.
211	
212	    Returns a decision dict with 'decision' key set to one of:
213	      emit_edge, reject_just_mention, escalate_cross_identity, escalate_disambiguation
214	
215	    Primary classification signal: `snippet` (200-char window around «target»).
216	    The snippet is far more reliable than scanning the full evidence_paragraph,
217	    which contains text about many other entities.
218	    """
219	    source_slug = row["source_slug"]
220	    target_slug = row["target_slug"]
221	    target_type = row.get("target_type", "")
222	    evidence_paragraph = row.get("evidence_paragraph", "")
223	    snippet = row.get("snippet", "")
224	    valid_types = set(row.get("valid_edge_types", []))
225	    staging_verbs = row.get("staging_verbs_present", [])
226	    candidate_kind = row.get("candidate_kind", "source_target")
227	    source_section = row.get("source_section", "")
228	
229	    ttop = _t(target_type)
230	
231	    # Hard guard: KNOWS is deprecated — never emit
232	    valid_types.discard("KNOWS")
233	
234	    # Build clean versions of both signals
235	    cs = _clean(snippet)         # cleaned snippet — primary signal
236	    cp = _clean(evidence_paragraph)  # cleaned paragraph — secondary/fallback
237	
238	    # Same-slug guard
239	    if source_slug == target_slug:
240	        return _reject(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug,
241	                       "same-slug-source-equals-target")
242	
243	    # Source-in-snippet guard: check whether source name appears in snippet.
244	    # Used by relationship types that describe a direct source↔target bond
245	    # (kinship, service, emotional) where false positives come from the snippet
246	    # describing OTHER entities' relationships.
247	    source_in_snip = _source_in_snippet(source_slug, cs)
248	
249	    # Split snippet around the «target» anchor for directionality checks.
250	    # `snip_before` = text before the target anchor (source's actions toward target)
251	    # `snip_after`  = text after the target anchor (target's attributes/actions)
252	    snip_before, snip_target, snip_after = _split_snippet_at_target(snippet)
253	
254	    # Dispatch by target type
255	    if ttop == "character":
256	        return _classify_character(
257	            cs, cp, source_slug, target_slug, target_type, evidence_paragraph,
258	            valid_types, staging_verbs, candidate_kind, source_section,
259	            source_in_snip, snip_before, snip_after
260	        )
261	    if ttop == "place":
262	        return _classify_place(
263	            cs, cp, source_slug, target_slug, target_type, evidence_paragraph,
264	            valid_types, candidate_kind, source_section, source_in_snip
265	        )
266	    if ttop == "organization":
267	        return _classify_org(
268	            cs, cp, source_slug, target_slug, target_type, evidence_paragraph,
269	            valid_types, candidate_kind, source_section, source_in_snip
270	        )
271	    if ttop == "event":
272	        return _classify_event(
273	            cs, cp, source_slug, target_slug, target_type, evidence_paragraph,
274	            valid_types, candidate_kind, source_section, source_in_snip
275	        )
276	    if target_type == "title":
277	        return _classify_title(
278	            cs, cp, source_slug, target_slug, evidence_paragraph,
279	            valid_types, candidate_kind, source_section, source_in_snip
280	        )
281	    if ttop == "concept":
282	        return _classify_concept(
283	            cs, cp, source_slug, target_slug, target_type, evidence_paragraph,
284	            valid_types, candidate_kind, source_section, source_in_snip
285	        )
286	    if ttop == "object":
287	        return _classify_object(
288	            cs, cp, source_slug, target_slug, target_type, evidence_paragraph,
289	            valid_types, candidate_kind, source_section, source_in_snip
290	        )
291	    if target_type == "species":
292	        if "CULTURE_OF" in valid_types:
293	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "CULTURE_OF",
294	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=2)
295	        return _reject(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug,
296	                       "no-fitting-type-vocab-locked")
297	
298	    return _reject(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug,
299	                   f"unknown-target-type:{target_type}")
300	
301	
302	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
303	# Per-target-type classifiers — all operate on `cs` (cleaned snippet) primary
304	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
305	
306	def _classify_character(
307	    cs, cp, source_slug, target_slug, target_type, evidence_paragraph,
308	    valid_types, staging_verbs, candidate_kind, source_section,
309	    source_in_snip=True, snip_before="", snip_after=""
310	):
311	    """
312	    Classify edges toward character.* targets using snippet as primary signal.
313	
314	    source_in_snip: True if the source entity's first name appears in the snippet.
315	    snip_before: cleaned text appearing BEFORE the «target» anchor — used for
316	                 directionality checks (subject-verb-object ordering).
317	    snip_after:  cleaned text appearing AFTER the «target» anchor.
318	
319	    Kinship and direct-relationship types guard on source_in_snip to avoid
320	    matching relationship words that describe OTHER entities' relationships
321	    within the same paragraph window.
322	
323	    Directional types (KILLS, COMMANDS, PARENT_OF) additionally require the
324	    action verb to appear in snip_before (before the target anchor), ensuring
325	    the source is the AGENT of the action, not the patient or bystander.
326	    """
327	
328	    # ENCOUNTERS: only with staging verb pre-detected by enricher
329	    if staging_verbs and "ENCOUNTERS" in valid_types:
330	        return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "ENCOUNTERS",
331	                     evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=3)
332	
333	    # For kinship and direct personal-relationship types, only classify when
334	    # the source entity appears in the snippet. If the source isn't in the
335	    # snippet, the relationship words describe OTHER characters' relationships
336	    # within the shared paragraph context.
337	    kin_guard = source_in_snip
338	
339	    # Kinship — look in snippet for possessive pronouns or direct relationship words
340	    # near the target anchor. Patterns like "his brother X", "X, nephew of Y", etc.
341	
342	    # Spouse: guard with source presence because "husband/wife" often describes
343	    # third parties' marriages in shared paragraph context
344	    if kin_guard and _any(cs, "wife", "husband", "married to", "spouse of",
345	                          "wed to", "widow of", "wedded"):
346	        if "SPOUSE_OF" in valid_types:
347	            qual = "widowed" if "widow" in cs else "current"
348	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "SPOUSE_OF",
349	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=1, qualifier=qual)
350	        if "BETROTHED_TO" in valid_types and _any(cs, "betrothed", "promised in"):
351	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "BETROTHED_TO",
352	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=2, qualifier="current")
353	
354	    # Parent / child — source→child: action words appear BEFORE target anchor
355	    # Use snip_before to verify the source is the subject, not just present
356	    if kin_guard and _any(snip_before, "his son", "her son", "his daughter",
357	                          "her daughter", "his child", "her child", "father of",
358	                          "mother of", "fathered", "bore him", "bore her",
359	                          "gave birth", "sired", "bastard son", "bastard daughter"):
360	        if "PARENT_OF" in valid_types:
361	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "PARENT_OF",
362	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=1,
363	                         qualifier="biological")
364	
365	    # Parent appearing as attribute of target (target's parent is source):
366	    # "his father [target]" / "her mother [target]" — relationship word before anchor
367	    if kin_guard and _any(snip_before, "his father", "her father", "his mother",
368	                          "her mother", "his parent", "her parent"):
369	        if "PARENT_OF" in valid_types:
370	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "PARENT_OF",
371	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=1,
372	                         qualifier="biological")
373	
374	    # Sibling — explicit half/full forms; do NOT include "sworn brother" here
375	    # (sworn brother = Night's Watch / Kingsguard brotherhood, not blood kin)
376	    if _any(cs, "half-brother", "half brother"):
377	        if "SIBLING_OF" in valid_types:
378	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "SIBLING_OF",
379	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=1, qualifier="half")
380	    if _any(cs, "half-sister", "half sister"):
381	        if "SIBLING_OF" in valid_types:
382	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "SIBLING_OF",
383	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=1, qualifier="half")
384	    if kin_guard and _any(cs, " his brother", " her brother", "brother of",
385	                          "twin brother", "elder brother", "younger brother"):
386	        if "SIBLING_OF" in valid_types:
387	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "SIBLING_OF",
388	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=1, qualifier="full")
389	    if kin_guard and _any(cs, " his sister", " her sister", "sister of",
390	                          "elder sister", "younger sister", "twin sister"):
391	        if "SIBLING_OF" in valid_types:
392	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "SIBLING_OF",
393	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=1, qualifier="full")
394	
395	    # Uncle / nephew / aunt / niece — guard on source presence
396	    if kin_guard and _any(cs, "his uncle", "her uncle", "uncle of",
397	                          "his aunt", "her aunt", "aunt of"):
398	        if "UNCLE_OF" in valid_types:
399	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "UNCLE_OF",
400	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=2)
401	    if kin_guard and _any(cs, "his nephew", "her nephew", "nephew of",
402	                          "his niece", "her niece", "niece of"):
403	        if "NEPHEW_OF" in valid_types:
404	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "NEPHEW_OF",
405	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=2)
406	
407	    # Cousin — guard on source presence
408	    if kin_guard and _any(cs, "his cousin", "her cousin", "cousin of", "their cousin"):
409	        if "COUSIN_OF" in valid_types:
410	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "COUSIN_OF",
411	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=2)
412	
413	    # Betrothal — guard on source presence
414	    if kin_guard and _any(cs, "betrothed", "betroth", "promised in marriage",
415	                          "his bride", "her groom"):
416	        if "BETROTHED_TO" in valid_types:
417	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "BETROTHED_TO",
418	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=2, qualifier="current")
419	
420	    # Lover / paramour — guard on source presence
421	    if kin_guard and _any(cs, "his lover", "her lover", "his paramour", "her paramour",
422	                          "his mistress", "her mistress", "paramour"):
423	        if "LOVER_OF" in valid_types:
424	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "LOVER_OF",
425	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=2, qualifier="current")
426	
427	    # Ward / fostered — guard on source presence
428	    if kin_guard and _any(cs, "his ward", "her ward", "ward of", "fostered by",
429	                          "raised by", "taken as ward", "taken in as ward"):
430	        if "WARD_OF" in valid_types:
431	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "WARD_OF",
432	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=1, qualifier="formal")
433	
434	    # Ancestor / heir
435	    if kin_guard and _any(cs, "ancestor", "descended from", "descendant of"):
436	        if "ANCESTOR_OF" in valid_types:
437	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "ANCESTOR_OF",
438	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=2)
439	    if kin_guard and _any(cs, "heir to", "his heir", "her heir", "named heir",
440	                          "designated heir"):
441	        if "HEIR_TO" in valid_types:
442	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "HEIR_TO",
443	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=1)
444	
445	    # In-law — guard on source presence
446	    if kin_guard and _any(cs, "good-mother", "good-father", "good-brother",
447	                          "good-sister", "mother-in-law", "father-in-law",
448	                          "sister-in-law", "brother-in-law",
449	                          "son-in-law", "daughter-in-law", "good mother", "good father"):
450	        if "IN_LAW_OF" in valid_types:
451	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "IN_LAW_OF",
452	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=2, qualifier="unknown")
453	
454	    # Squire / service — guard on source presence to avoid "assigned as squire to X"
455	    # matching when the subject is a different character
456	    if source_in_snip and _any(cs, "his squire", "her squire", "squire to",
457	                                "squired for", "squired to", "serves as squire",
458	                                "assigned as squire", "as his squire", "as her squire"):
459	        if "SERVES" in valid_types:
460	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "SERVES",
461	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=1)
462	
463	    # All remaining character→character relationship types require source_in_snip.
464	    # The snippet window is centered on the TARGET; if the source entity's first name
465	    # isn't in that window, the relationship word describes OTHER characters'
466	    # relationships and we cannot safely attribute it to source→target.
467	    if not source_in_snip:
468	        return _reject(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug,
469	                       "source-not-in-snippet-reject")
470	
471	    # --- below here source_in_snip is guaranteed True ---
472	
473	    # Knighthood
474	    if _any(cs, "knighted by", "dubbed by", "dubbing", "made a knight by",
475	            "received knighthood from"):
476	        if "KNIGHTED_BY" in valid_types:
477	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "KNIGHTED_BY",
478	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=1)
479	        if "BESTOWS_KNIGHTHOOD_ON" in valid_types:
480	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug,
481	                         "BESTOWS_KNIGHTHOOD_ON",
482	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=1)
483	
484	    # Killing / death — kill verbs must appear BEFORE the target anchor AND
485	    # the source's first name must also appear in snip_before (to confirm that
486	    # the source is the grammatical subject of the kill, not a bystander).
487	    source_first = _source_name_parts(source_slug)[0] if _source_name_parts(source_slug) else ""
488	    source_is_killer = (source_first and source_first in snip_before)
489	
490	    if source_is_killer and _any(snip_before, " killed ", " kills ", " slew ",
491	                                  " slain ", " slays ", "cut down", "struck down",
492	                                  "beheads", "beheaded", "murdered", "murders",
493	                                  "put to death"):
494	        if _any(cs, "poison", "poisons", "poisoned", "strangles") and "POISONS" in valid_types:
495	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "POISONS",
496	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=1)
497	        if "KILLS" in valid_types:
498	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "KILLS",
499	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=1,
500	                         qualifier="in_combat")
501	        if "EXECUTES" in valid_types and _any(snip_before, "executed", "executes"):

733	def _classify_place(
734	    cs, cp, source_slug, target_slug, target_type, evidence_paragraph,
735	    valid_types, candidate_kind, source_section, source_in_snip=True
736	):
737	    """
738	    Classify edges toward place.* targets.
739	    Source-presence guard on all types except LOCATED_AT broad fallback,
740	    which is intentionally permissive (presence in context implies location).
741	    However, even LOCATED_AT requires source_in_snip to avoid attributing
742	    location to a character merely mentioned in passing.
743	    """
744	    if not source_in_snip:
745	        return _reject(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug,
746	                       "source-not-in-snippet-reject")
747	
748	    if _any(cs, "born in", "born at", "birthplace", "was born"):
749	        if "BORN_AT" in valid_types:
750	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "BORN_AT",
751	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=1)
752	    if _any(cs, "died at", "died in", "death at", "killed at",
753	            "killed in", "fell at", "died near"):
754	        if "DIED_AT" in valid_types:
755	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "DIED_AT",
756	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=1)
757	    if _any(cs, "buried at", "buried in", "interred at", "tomb at",
758	            "tomb in", "crypt at", "crypt of"):
759	        if "BURIED_AT" in valid_types:
760	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "BURIED_AT",
761	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=1)
762	    if _any(cs, "imprisoned at", "imprisoned in", "held at", "held in",
763	            "locked in", "locked away in", "confined to", "confined in"):
764	        if "IMPRISONED_AT" in valid_types:
765	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "IMPRISONED_AT",
766	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=3)
767	    if _any(cs, "traveled to", "travels to", "journeyed to", "rode to",
768	            "sailed to", "went to", "fled to", "marched to", "proceeded to",
769	            "arrived at", "arrived in", "returned to", "departed for"):
770	        if "TRAVELS_TO" in valid_types:
771	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "TRAVELS_TO",
772	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=2)
773	    if _any(cs, " rules ", " ruled ", "lord of", "lady of", "king of", "queen of",
774	            "holds dominion", "seat of rule"):
775	        if "RULES" in valid_types:
776	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "RULES",
777	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=1)
778	    if _any(cs, "besieges", "besieged", "laid siege to"):
779	        if "BESIEGES" in valid_types:
780	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "BESIEGES",
781	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=1)
782	    if _any(cs, " built ", "constructed", "oversaw construction", "had built"):
783	        if "BUILT" in valid_types:
784	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "BUILT",
785	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=1)
786	    # Generic location presence — broad fallback for place targets
787	    if "LOCATED_AT" in valid_types:
788	        return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "LOCATED_AT",
789	                     evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=3)
790	    return _reject(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug,
791	                   "no-fitting-type-vocab-locked")

874	def _classify_event(
875	    cs, cp, source_slug, target_slug, target_type, evidence_paragraph,
876	    valid_types, candidate_kind, source_section, source_in_snip=True
877	):
878	    """
879	    Classify edges toward event.* targets. Uses both snippet and paragraph.
880	
881	    For events, the snippet window often cuts off the action verb, so we also
882	    check the full paragraph (cp) as secondary signal. However, the broad
883	    fallbacks (FIGHTS_IN/PARTICIPATES_IN via paragraph scan) only fire when
884	    the source entity appears in the snippet, to avoid attributing participation
885	    in events described in surrounding context.
886	    """
887	    combined = cs + " " + cp
888	
889	    if _any(combined, "fought in", "fought at", "fighting in",
890	            "took part in battle", "joined the battle", "participated in the battle",
891	            "in the battle", "during the battle"):
892	        if "FIGHTS_IN" in valid_types:
893	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "FIGHTS_IN",
894	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=1)
895	
896	    if _any(combined, " commanded ", " commanded in ", " led the ", " led forces",
897	            "general at", "commanded forces"):
898	        if "COMMANDS_IN" in valid_types:
899	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "COMMANDS_IN",
900	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=1)
901	
902	    if _any(combined, "attended", " attends ", "present at", "witnessed",
903	            "guest at", "spectator", "observed the"):
904	        if "ATTENDS" in valid_types:
905	            return _emit(candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, "ATTENDS",
906	                         evidence_paragraph, source_section, tier=2)
907	
908	    if _any(combined, "officiated", "officiates", "performed the ceremony",
909	            "conducted the", "presided over"):

1085	def _snippet(evidence_paragraph: str, min_len: int = 10, max_len: int = 200) -> str:
1086	    """
1087	    Extract a verbatim snippet from evidence_paragraph, 10-200 chars.
1088	    Strips «» wiki anchor markers but preserves all other text verbatim.
1089	    """
1090	    text = evidence_paragraph.strip()
1091	    text = text.replace("«", "").replace("»", "")
1092	    if len(text) <= max_len:
1093	        return text
1094	    # Truncate at word boundary near max_len
1095	    truncated = text[:max_len]
1096	    last_space = truncated.rfind(" ")
1097	    if last_space > min_len:
1098	        truncated = truncated[:last_space]
1099	    return truncated

1103	          edge_type: str, evidence_paragraph: str, evidence_section: str,
1104	          tier: int, qualifier=None) -> dict:
1105	    """Build an emit_edge decision row with correct qualifier tier handling."""
1106	    row: dict = {
1107	        "decision": "emit_edge",
1108	        "candidate_kind": candidate_kind,
1109	        "evidence_kind": "wiki-entity",
1110	        "source_slug": source_slug,
1111	        "target_slug": target_slug,
1112	        "edge_type": edge_type,
1113	        "evidence_snippet": _snippet(evidence_paragraph),
1114	        "evidence_section": evidence_section,
1115	        "confidence_tier": tier,
1116	    }
1117	
1118	    # Qualifier enforcement
1119	    if edge_type in TIER1_QUALIFIERS:
1120	        # Required: use provided or fall back to "unknown"
1121	        if qualifier is not None and qualifier in TIER1_QUALIFIERS[edge_type]:
1122	            row["qualifier"] = qualifier
1123	        else:
1124	            row["qualifier"] = "unknown"
1125	    elif edge_type in TIER2_QUALIFIERS:
1126	        # Optional: include only if valid
1127	        if qualifier is not None and qualifier in TIER2_QUALIFIERS[edge_type]:
1128	            row["qualifier"] = qualifier
1129	        # else: omit — Tier 2 permits missing qualifier
1130	    # Tier 3: no qualifier field — never add one
1131	
1132	    return row

1148	# File processing

1150	
1151	def process_file(input_path: Path, output_path: Path, dry_run: bool) -> dict:
1152	    """Read input JSONL, classify each candidate, write output JSONL."""
1153	    if not input_path.exists():
1154	        print(f"  [WARN] Input not found: {input_path}", file=sys.stderr)
1155	        return {"input": str(input_path), "error": "file-not-found"}
1156	
1157	    candidates = []
1158	    with open(input_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
1159	        for lineno, line in enumerate(fh, 1):
1160	            line = line.strip()
1161	            if not line:
1162	                continue
1163	            try:
1164	                candidates.append(json.loads(line))
1165	            except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
1166	                print(f"  [WARN] JSON parse error at line {lineno} of "
1167	                      f"{input_path.name}: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
1168	
1169	    decisions = [classify_candidate(row) for row in candidates]
1170	
1171	    counts: dict = {}
1172	    for d in decisions:
1173	        key = d["decision"]
1174	        counts[key] = counts.get(key, 0) + 1
1175	
1176	    if not dry_run:
1177	        output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
1178	        with open(output_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
1179	            for d in decisions:
1180	                fh.write(json.dumps(d, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
1181	
1182	    return {
1183	        "input": input_path.name,
1184	        "output": output_path.name,
1185	        "total": len(decisions),
1186	        "counts": counts,
1187	    }
1188	
1189	
1190	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1191	# Entry point
1192	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

1194	def main():
1195	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
1196	        description="Stage 4 prose-edge classifier — deterministic rule-based pass."
1197	    )
1198	    parser.add_argument(
1199	        "--dry-run", action="store_true",
1200	        help="Classify but do not write output files."
1201	    )
1202	    args = parser.parse_args()
1203	
1204	    if args.dry_run:
1205	        print("[dry-run] Classifying without writing output files.\n")
1206	
1207	    all_stats = []
1208	    for input_path, output_path in FILE_PAIRS:
1209	        stats = process_file(input_path, output_path, dry_run=args.dry_run)
1210	        all_stats.append(stats)
1211	
1212	    print()
1213	    print("=" * 70)
1214	    print("Stage 4 Prose-Edge Classifier — Summary")
1215	    print("=" * 70)
1216	
1217	    total_candidates = 0
1218	    total_emitted = 0
1219	    total_rejected = 0
1220	    total_escalated = 0
1221	
1222	    for s in all_stats:
1223	        if "error" in s:
1224	            print(f"  [ERROR] {s['input']}: {s['error']}")
1225	            continue
1226	
1227	        counts = s["counts"]
1228	        emitted = counts.get("emit_edge", 0)
1229	        rejected = counts.get("reject_just_mention", 0)
1230	        escalated = (counts.get("escalate_cross_identity", 0)
1231	                     + counts.get("escalate_disambiguation", 0))
1232	
1233	        total_candidates += s["total"]
1234	        total_emitted += emitted
1235	        total_rejected += rejected
1236	        total_escalated += escalated
1237	
1238	        parts = []
1239	        if emitted:
1240	            parts.append(f"{emitted} emit_edge")
1241	        if rejected:
1242	            parts.append(f"{rejected} reject_just_mention")
1243	        if escalated:
1244	            parts.append(f"{escalated} escalate")
1245	
1246	        suffix = f" → {s['output']}" if not args.dry_run else ""
1247	        print(f"  [done] {s['input']!s} → {', '.join(parts)}{suffix}")
1248	
1249	    print()
1250	    print(f"  Total candidates : {total_candidates}")
1251	    print(f"  Emitted edges    : {total_emitted}")
1252	    print(f"  Rejected         : {total_rejected}")

import json, re

_CITE_REF_RE = re.compile(r'\(wiki:[^)]+\)')
_ANGLE_QUOTE_RE = re.compile(r'[«»]')

def _clean(text):
    text = _WIKI_LINK_RE.sub(r'\1', text)
    text = _CITE_REF_RE.sub('', text)
    text = _ANGLE_QUOTE_RE.sub('', text)
    return text.lower()

def _source_name_parts(slug):
    return [p for p in slug.split('-') if len(p) >= 4]

def _source_in_snippet(source_slug, snippet_clean):
    parts = _source_name_parts(source_slug)
    if not parts:
        return True
    first_name = parts[0]
    return first_name in snippet_clean

input.new_string:
_WIKI_LINK_RE = re.compile(r'\[([^\]]+)\]\(wiki:[^)]+\)')
# Match complete (wiki:...) citations AND truncated ones ending with '...' or end-of-string.
# Snippets are 200-char windows and may be cut mid-citation, leaving "(wiki:Foo.cite..." with
# no closing ')'. The second alternative strips these partial refs so they don't leak entity
# names (e.g. "podrick" in "(wiki:Podrick_Payne.cite_ref...") into the cleaned text.
_CITE_REF_RE = re.compile(r'\(wiki:[^)]*\)|\(wiki:[^)]*$|\(wiki:[^)]*\.\.\.')
_ANGLE_QUOTE_RE = re.compile(r'[«»]')


def _clean(text: str) -> str:
    """
    Strip wiki markup from snippet/paragraph text and return lowercased plain text.

    # Require the reveal verb in snip_before (the source acts toward the target)
    # OR in the full snippet for passive forms. Do NOT emit on "tells him/her"

                              "told her", "informed him", "informed her",

    if _reveals_in_before and not _reveals_in_after:
        if "REVEALS_TO" in valid_types:

import json, re

_WIKI_LINK_RE = re.compile(r'\[([^\]]+)\]\(wiki:[^)]+\)')
_CITE_REF_RE = re.compile(r'\(wiki:[^)]*\)|\(wiki:[^)]*$|\(wiki:[^)]*\.\.\.')
_ANGLE_QUOTE_RE = re.compile(r'[«»]')

def _clean(text):
    text = _WIKI_LINK_RE.sub(r'\1', text)
    text = _CITE_REF_RE.sub('', text)
    text = _ANGLE_QUOTE_RE.sub('', text)
    return text.lower()

path = '/Users/mnoth/source/asoiaf-chat/working/wiki/pass2-buckets/characters-house-payne/prose-edge-candidates-enriched/podrick-payne.candidates.jsonl'
with open(path) as f:
    rows = [json.loads(l) for l in f if l.strip()]

for r in rows:
    if r.get('target_slug') in ['battle-on-the-green-fork', 'cedric-payne']:
        snip = r.get('snippet','')
        cs = _clean(snip)
        sin = 'podrick' in cs
        print(f'TARGET={r[\"target_slug\"]}  sin={sin}')
        print(f'  CS={repr(cs[:150])}')
        cp = _clean(r.get('evidence_paragraph',''))
        print(f'  CP BATTLE TERMS: battle={\"battle\" in cp}, fought={\"fought\" in cp}, during={\"during\" in cp}')
        # possessive check
        source_first2 = 'podrick'
        source_possessive = source_first2 + \"'s\"
        ev_target = r['target_slug'].replace('-',' ')
        if ev_target in cs:
            ev_before = cs.split(ev_target,1)[0]
        else:
            ev_before = cs
        poss_fam = any(source_possessive+x in ev_before for x in [' father',' mother',' brother',' sister',' uncle',' son',' daughter'])
        print(f'  poss_fam_in_before={poss_fam}')
        print()
"

import json

for name, path in [
    ('amaury-peake', '/Users/mnoth/source/asoiaf-chat/working/wiki/pass2-buckets/characters-house-peake/prose-edges-haiku/amaury-peake.edges.jsonl'),
    ('bernard', '/Users/mnoth/source/asoiaf-chat/working/wiki/pass2-buckets/characters-house-peake/prose-edges-haiku/bernard.edges.jsonl'),

]:
    with open(path) as f:
        rows = [json.loads(l) for l in f if l.strip()]
    emits = [r for r in rows if r.get('decision') == 'emit_edge']
    print(f'=== {name} ({len(emits)} emits) ===')
    for e in emits:
        q = e.get('qualifier','')
        print(f'  {e[\"edge_type\"]:25} {e[\"target_slug\"]:35} {q}')
    print()
"

python3 -c "
import re

_WIKI_LINK_RE = re.compile(r'\[([^\]]+)\]\(wiki:[^)]+\)')
_CITE_REF_RE = re.compile(r'\(wiki:[^)]*\)|\(wiki:[^)]*$|\(wiki:[^)]*\.\.\.')
_ANGLE_QUOTE_RE = re.compile(r'[«»]')

def _clean(text):
    text = _WIKI_LINK_RE.sub(r'\1', text)
    text = _CITE_REF_RE.sub('', text)
    text = _ANGLE_QUOTE_RE.sub('', text)
    return text.lower()

snip1 = '...o had allied with the [Sealord of Braavos](wiki:Sealord_of_Braavos) and the «Archon of Tyrosh» , Gedmund remained at [Tarth](wiki:Tarth) to await further instructions fro...'
snip2 = '...\"Gedmund Green-Sick\". When it was learned that Racallio had allied with the «Sealord of Braavos» and the [Archon of Tyrosh](wiki:Archon_of_Tyrosh), Gedmund remained at [Tar..'

for snip, label in [(snip1,'archon'), (snip2,'sealord')]:
    cs = _clean(snip)
    sin = 'gedmund' in cs
    print(f'{label}: gedmund in cs = {sin}')
    print(f'  CS={repr(cs[:150])}')
    if '«' in snip:
        start = snip.find('«')
        end = snip.find('»', start)
        snip_before = _clean(snip[:start])
        print(f'  SNIP_BEFORE={repr(snip_before[:80])}')
        print(f'  gedmund in snip_before: {\"gedmund\" in snip_before}')
    print()
"

import json, re

_WIKI_LINK_RE = re.compile(r'\[([^\]]+)\]\(wiki:[^)]+\)')
_CITE_REF_RE = re.compile(r'\(wiki:[^)]*\)|\(wiki:[^)]*$|\(wiki:[^)]*\.\.\.')
_ANGLE_QUOTE_RE = re.compile(r'[«»]')

def _clean(text):
    text = _WIKI_LINK_RE.sub(r'\1', text)
    text = _CITE_REF_RE.sub('', text)
    text = _ANGLE_QUOTE_RE.sub('', text)
    return text.lower()

path = '/Users/mnoth/source/asoiaf-chat/working/wiki/pass2-buckets/characters-house-peake/prose-edge-candidates-enriched/gedmund-peake.candidates.jsonl'

        cs = _clean(snip)
        sin = 'gedmund' in cs
        if '«' in snip:

            end = snip.find('»', start)

        print('SNIP_RAW:', repr(snip[:200]))

content.text:
The classifier is stable. Here's a summary of what was fixed in this session:

**Bugs fixed:**

1. **Partial citation ref leak** — `(wiki:Podrick_Payne.cite_ref...)` truncated snippets leaked entity names into cleaned text. Fixed `_CITE_REF_RE` to strip partial citations ending with `...` or at string end.

2. **PARENT_OF/SPOUSE_OF third-party fire** — "Larra Rogare, wife of prince «Viserys»" triggered SPOUSE_OF for Septon Bernard. "gave birth to prince «Aegon», Septon Bernard anointed..." triggered PARENT_OF for Bernard. Fixed by requiring source's first name in `snip_before` for both PARENT_OF and SPOUSE_OF.

3. **HOLDS_TITLE without source linked to title** — "Gedmund remained... the «Archon of Tyrosh»" fired HOLDS_TITLE for Gedmund. Fixed by requiring either source in snip_before OR explicit title-holding language.

4. **HOLDS_TITLE possessive** — "The Hand's squire" fired HOLDS_TITLE hand-of-the-king for Podrick. Fixed by detecting possessive `target_base + "'s"` in snippet.

5. **HOLDS_TITLE third-party** — "his father was a «squire»" fired HOLDS_TITLE squire for Podrick. Fixed by detecting third-party subject patterns in snip_before.

6. **SERVES hand-of-the-king** — "the hand's squire, Lord Tywin?" triggered SERVES tywin-lannister via "hand of" phrase. Fixed by removing bare "hand of" from SERVES triggers; only match "hand of the" when "hand's" (possessive) not present.

7. **SERVES denial context** — "I'm his squire... Lord Tywin? No. Not that Hand." triggered SERVES tywin-lannister. Fixed by detecting denial context ("no. not that") before squire→SERVES emission.

8. **FIGHTS_IN recollection** — "Tyrion recalls that Podrick had been sleeping before the «second siege of Meereen»" triggered FIGHTS_IN. Fixed with two-part recollection guard: (a) "recalls that" before event anchor, (b) "recalls that [source]" after event anchor.

9. **FIGHTS_IN family-member participation** — "Podrick's father died in «Greyjoy's Rebellion»" triggered FIGHTS_IN. Fixed by detecting source-possessive family member before event anchor.

10. **REVEALS_TO direction** — "Brella tells him that..." with «Brella» as target triggered REVEALS_TO Brella. "tells him" in snip_after means TARGET tells SOURCE, not vice versa. Fixed by requiring reveals verb in snip_before and not in snip_after.

11. **COUSIN_OF/NEPHEW_OF/UNCLE_OF from snip_after** — "his cousin Bernard" after «Eustace» anchor triggered COUSIN_OF Eustace. Fixed by requiring these kinship patterns in snip_before only.

12. **Year-slug guard** — "134-ac" misclassified as character.human triggered NEPHEW_OF. Fixed with year-slug pattern rejection.

The script is at `/Users/mnoth/source/asoiaf-chat/scripts/stage4-prose-edge-classifier.py`.

Output files:
- `/Users/mnoth/source/asoiaf-chat/working/wiki/pass2-buckets/characters-house-payne/prose-edges-haiku/podrick-payne.edges.jsonl` — 12 emit_edge, 85 reject

You are building a Python script to classify all Stage 4 prose-edge candidates for 5 ASOIAF character files.

## What you're building

A Stage 4 prose-edge classifier that will read 5 enriched candidates JSONL files and emit 5 edge JSONL files with decisions for each candidate.

## Input file paths (read-only):
1. working/wiki/pass2-buckets/characters-house-payne/prose-edge-candidates-enriched/podrick-payne.candidates.jsonl (97 candidates)
2. working/wiki/pass2-buckets/characters-house-peake/prose-edge-candidates-enriched/amaury-peake.candidates.jsonl (23 candidates)
3. working/wiki/source/asoiaf-chat/working/wiki/pass2-buckets/characters-house-peake/prose-edge-candidates-enriched/armen-peake.candidates.jsonl (7 candidates)
4. working/wiki/pass2-buckets/characters-house-peake/prose-edge-candidates-enriched/bernard.candidates.jsonl (28 candidates)
5. working/wiki/pass2-buckets/characters-house-peake/prose-edge-candidates-enriched/gedmund-peake.candidates.jsonl (21 candidates)

## Output file paths (write):
1. working/wiki/pass2-buckets/characters-house-payne/prose-edges-haiku/podrick-payne.edges.jsonl
2. working/wiki/pass2-buckets/characters-house-peake/prose-edges-haiku/amaury-peake.edges.jsonl
3. working/wiki/pass2-buckets/characters-house-peake/prose-edges-haiku/armen-peake.edges.jsonl
4. working/wiki/pass2-buckets/characters-house-peake/prose-edges-haiku/bernard.edges.jsonl
5. working/wiki/pass2-buckets/characters-house-peake/prose-edges-haiku/gedmund-peake.edges.jsonl

## Classifier rules (session 64 locked vocabulary + hard constraints):

1. **ENCOUNTERS requires explicit staging verb**: `staging_verbs_present` field contains Python-detected verbs. Emit ENCOUNTERS ONLY if the field is non-empty AND the verb actually stages a source-target meeting.

2. **Rule: KNOWS is deprecated (Session 63)** — never emit. Reject with `knows-deprecated-defer-to-pass1`.

3. **Type contracts** are pre-computed per candidate in the `valid_edge_types` field. Pick ONLY from that list.

4. **For each candidate, decide ONE of:**
   - `emit_edge` — pick `edge_type` from `valid_edge_types`. Copy `evidence_paragraph` into `evidence_snippet`. Set `confidence_tier` to 1 (explicit), 2 (inferred/clear), or 3 (hinted).
   - `reject_just_mention` — entity mentioned but no edge. Reason field required.
   - `escalate_cross_identity` — candidate slugs refer to same person under different names.
   - `escalate_disambiguation` — target slug ambiguous (multiple entities possible).

5. **Tier 1 edges REQUIRE `qualifier`** from enum (see locked vocab). If not in locked vocab for that type, use unknown.

6. **Evidence snippet** must be verbatim from `evidence_paragraph`, 10-200 chars.

7. **Pattern avoidance:**
   - Don't emit CONTEMPORARY_WITH as fallback for character pairs with no fitting type.
   - Don't emit ENCOUNTERS without staging verb.
   - Don't emit ATTENDS toward persons (target must be event).
   - Don't emit FIGHTS_IN toward persons (target must be event).

## Key fields in each candidate row:
- `candidate_kind`: "source_target" (all 5 files are source_target)
- `source_slug`, `target_slug`: entity slugs
- `target_type`: target node type (character.human, place.location, event.battle, organization.*, etc.)
- `evidence_paragraph`: full prose supporting the edge
- `valid_edge_types`: list of types allowed for this target_type
- `staging_verbs_present`: list of detected ENCOUNTERS-verb matches (or empty list)
- `source_section`, `snippet`: for evidence citation

## Write the script to:
1. Read each input file as JSONL (one dict per line)
2. For each candidate row, emit ONE decision row to the corresponding output JSONL
3. Create output directories if missing
4. Use the decision schema from the classifier manual (Output Contract):
   - `emit_edge`: decision, candidate_kind, evidence_kind="wiki-entity", source_slug, target_slug, edge_type, evidence_snippet, evidence_section, confidence_tier
   - `reject_just_mention`: decision, candidate_kind, source_slug, target_slug, reason
   - etc.
5. Print a summary per file: "[done] podrick-payne.candidates.jsonl → 87 emit_edge, 10 reject_just_mention → podrick-payne.edges.jsonl"

## Important constraints:
- Do NOT read source or target node files.
- Use only the enriched candidate row fields.
- Preserve `candidate_kind` as a discriminator in every output row.
- All emit_edge rows set `evidence_kind: "wiki-entity"`.
- No manual reasoning outside the locked vocab — if no fitting type exists in `valid_edge_types`, reject_just_mention.
- The locked vocabulary is ~163 types (inlined in the prose-edge-classifier.md manual). Pick ONLY from valid_edge_types.

## Edge type decision rules (summary):

Common types in wiki-prose:
- TUTORS, TEACHES, HEALS, PROTECTS, TRUSTS, FEARS, LOVES, HATES, RESPECTS, RESENTS, MOURNS, DISTRUSTS, PERCEIVED_AS (character perceptions)
- MEMBER_OF, FOUNDED, SERVES, COMMANDS, ADVISES, RULES, SWORN_TO, OVERLORD_OF, APPOINTS, HOLDS_TITLE, KNIGHTED_BY, BESTOWS_KNIGHTHOOD_ON (authority/faction)
- WIELDS, OWNS, LOCATED_AT, TRAVELED_TO, BORN_AT, DIED_AT, BURIED_AT (spatial/possession)
- KILLS, KILLED_BY, EXECUTES, POISONS, CAPTURES, DEFEATS, ATTACKS, DUELS, FIGHTS_IN (combat)
- SIBLING_OF, PARENT_OF, SPOUSE_OF, BETROTHED_TO, LOVER_OF, HEIR_TO, ANCESTOR_OF, COUSIN_OF (kinship)
- ALLIES_WITH, OPPOSES, BETRAYS, CONSPIRES_WITH, ALLIES_WITH, NEGOTIATES_WITH (factional)
- REVEALS_TO, DECEIVES, INFORMS, INVESTIGATES, SEEKS, HOARDS, IGNORANT_OF (knowledge)
- ATTENDS (person → event, not person), FIGHTS_IN (person → event), PARTICIPATES_IN (person → event), PART_OF (battle → war)
- ENCOUNTERS (face-to-face meeting with staging verb, both character.*)

DO NOT emit:
- KNOWS (deprecated)
- CONTEMPORARY_WITH as fallback (use reject_just_mention instead)
- ATTENDS, FIGHTS_IN, etc. toward persons (target must be event.*)

Write the script now.
